subject: Sociology
title: Bourdieu and Cultural Capital
description: A 60-second lesson on Bourdieu and Cultural Capital.

hook.text: Two students ace the exam. One knows which fork to use at a business dinner. Guess who gets the internship.

buildup.text: Pierre Bourdieu argued that class isn't only about money. Knowledge, tastes, and manners are also capital.

discovery.text: Cultural capital includes education, accent, taste in art, and knowing social codes — all learned from your class.

twist.text: Schools reward cultural capital they don't teach. Kids from educated homes arrive with advantages that look like talent.

climax.text: Bourdieu showed that inequality hides inside culture. It reproduces through taste, manners, and invisible social codes.

punchline.text: Class isn't just in your wallet. It's in your accent and tastes.

quiz.question: What is cultural capital?
options: Knowledge, tastes, and social skills learned from one's class
The number of books in a library
